coord_build: not_reported (genome_build not reported in source table; rs387907171 is a TYRP1 coding/missense variant, Kenny et al. 2012 Arg93Cys)
pvalue: P = 0.0014 (ANOVA, hair pigmentation, region-wide); P < 7.92e-13 with sex and age as covariates; among-island frequency differences chi-square = 206.5, df = 3, P < 0.001
population: Northern Island Melanesia (New Britain n=93 and Bougainville
evidence_quote: rs387907171 remains significantly, although weakly, associated with lighter hair pigmentation (F = 10.28, R2 = 0.0125, P = 0.0014). This association increases when sex and age (F = 20.68, R2 = 0.074, P < 7.92 x 10-13) are included as covariates.
classification_notes: TYRP1 canonical; rs387907171 = same R93C variant, dbSNP-identified later; population-genetics contribution, mechanism cited from Norton2014/Kenny2012.
author_explanation_quote: rs387907171 exhibits strong allele frequency differences among islands in Northern Island Melanesia. Its absence from Bougainville, as well as the weak association with decreased hair color, indicates that additional alleles contribute to the blondism phenotype
notes: rs387907171 is the dbSNP identifier for the same TYRP1 R93C nonsynonymous variant characterized mechanistically in Kenny2012 and Norton2014 (arginine-to-cysteine substitution); this short report's own contribution is a population-genetics finding (allele absent from Bougainville, weak residual association after adding this population) rather than a new molecular mechanism, but the coding-variant mechanism for the SNP itself is established and restated via citation to prior work. NOTE: contrary to the task prompt's characterization of Norton2016 as 'paywalled PDF-only with no supplement text' requiring honest-gaps handling, the downloaded main PDF is a complete 5-page short report (Abstract through Discussion/References) with no missing sections; no honest-gap needed for the classification field itself.
